bull_thesis: KDP is a sum-of-parts spin: management will separate into two U.S.-listed pure-plays — Beverage Co. (North American refreshment: Dr Pepper, Snapple, Canada Dry, Mott's, energy/sports) and Global Coffee Co. (Keurig single-serve + the ~$18B JDE Peet's platform acquired and CLOSED Apr 1 2026) — via a tax-free spin of Global Coffee Co. The enabling, binary leg (JDE close + ~$9B debt / ~$8.5B equity / ~$5B assumed bonds financing) has already fired cleanly. Both CEOs are named (Cofer → Beverage Co., Oliveira → Global Coffee Co.), org build-out is underway, and Q1 2026 reaffirmed low-double-digit EPS growth (EPS $1.35 +22.7%, rev TTM $16.94B +9.2%). On the spin's effective date the conglomerate discount collapses: a pure-play beverage staple re-rates toward consumer-staples comps and the coffee platform stands alone, plus mechanical S&P index-flow re-weighting forces passive buying across the two new tickers. Forward P/E ~12.9x today is a depressed blended multiple on a cash machine with a covered 3% yield. Win value ~ $37.70 on the SoP re-rate. Conditions: leverage must come down at both entities and equity/credit conditions must stay supportive into the spin window.
bear_thesis: The value-unlock event is UNDATED and serially right-shifting: management guides operational readiness end-2026 with official separation 'likely early 2027,' and — critically — timing is explicitly conditioned on 'appropriate leverage levels at each company AND supportive market conditions.' Combined net leverage is ~4.5x post-JDE; the spin is GATED on deleveraging that is rate- and FCF-dependent and can slip multiple quarters. The coffee category is currently a drag (JDE integration + soft global coffee), pressuring near-term EPS and the multiple. If the spin slips into late-2027 or stalls, the SoP discount persists and the holder waits 12–18+ months for a re-rate with no hard clock. The absolute spread is modest (~22% upside-to-target vs ~7.6% downside-to-floor; R:R ~1.9), thin compensation for an undated wait with a market-conditions out-clause. There is no PDUFA-style hard date, no fixed proxy/effective date, no Form 10 in hand.
sop_bull: Spin-win SoP ~ $37.70/sh. Derivation: separate the blended ~12.9x forward staple into (a) Beverage Co. — a high-quality NA refreshment pure-play deserving ~16–18x forward on its growth/margin profile — and (b) Global Coffee Co. (Keurig + JDE Peet's) — a global coffee platform re-rated to standalone coffee comps once the integration drag clears. Sum of the two pure-play equity values, net of separation/transaction costs and the elevated post-JDE leverage, plus mechanical index-inclusion flow on the effective date, implies ~$37–38/sh, i.e. removal of the conglomerate discount on a business already earning $1.35+ EPS and growing low-double-digits.
sop_bear: Verified downside floor ~ $28.50/sh. Derivation: the 'no-spin / stuck-conglomerate' standalone value. KDP remains a profitable consumer-staple at ~12.9x forward with a well-covered ~3% dividend, reaffirmed double-digit EPS growth, an active buyback, and non-core asset monetization optionality flagged by management as a deleveraging lever. A staples beverage business at a low-double-digit multiple with a safe yield does not collapse; ~$28.50 (≈7.6% below ~$30.85 live) is the bear/no-catalyst anchor where yield + earnings support kick in. Floor is REAL but THIN (~$2.35 of cushion).
sop_breakdown: Probability-weighted base case. Spin-win target $37.70 with ~55% odds the separation completes on a value-accretive basis within ~12–18 months (high — enabling leg fired, both CEOs named, financing closed) blended with the $28.50 no-spin/slip floor at ~45% (the undated 'likely early 2027' + leverage + market-conditions out-clause). 0.55*37.70 + 0.45*28.50 ≈ $33.56, ~$33.50. Parts: Beverage Co. pure-play re-rate to ~16–18x (the larger value driver) + Global Coffee Co. (Keurig + JDE Peet's) standalone once integration drag clears + mechanical S&P index-inclusion flow on the effective date, net of ~4.5x leverage and separation costs.
risk_reward: Upside-to-target ~22% ($30.85 → $37.70) vs downside-to-floor ~7.6% ($30.85 → $28.50). R:R ≈ 1.9:1 (dossier SoP R:R 1.87). Asymmetric and defined-risk, but the absolute spread is modest and the wait is undated.
catalyst_summary: Tax-free spin of Global Coffee Co. from Beverage Co. (index-flow SoP) — enabling JDE Peet's acquisition CLOSED Apr 1 2026, but the spin itself is UNDATED: 'operational readiness' end-2026, official separation 'likely early 2027,' gated on deleveraging (~4.5x) + 'supportive market conditions.' ~22% upside-to-target vs ~7.6% downside-to-floor (R:R ~1.9), no hard date.
dated_milestone: JDE Peet's acquisition CLOSED Apr 1, 2026 (hard leg fired). Spin: operational readiness targeted end-2026; official separation 'likely early 2027' per Q1 2026 call (Apr 23, 2026) — UNDATED, conditioned on leverage + supportive market conditions. No fixed record/effective/Form-10 date in hand.
forcing_function: The Global Coffee Co. spin record/effective date (the eventual Form 10 effectiveness + S&P index reconstitution that triggers mechanical passive re-weighting across the two new tickers). As of now this date is NOT SET — it is the missing hard clock; deleveraging below the gating threshold at both entities is the precondition that would convert this from SOFT to PENDING_HARD.
consensus_delta: The street treats KDP as a blended consumer-staples conglomerate at ~12.9x forward and prices the coffee drag as a persistent overhang (12-mo consensus PT ~$33.5, ~9% upside). The hidden thesis: the enabling JDE leg has ALREADY fired and the eventual tax-free spin mechanically removes the conglomerate discount and forces index-flow buying — a sum-of-parts the blended multiple does not capture. Where consensus is right (and the reason this is NOT an aggressive entry): the re-rate is UNDATED and management has serially right-shifted it toward 'early 2027,' so the market's refusal to pay for the spin today is rational, not an error — the spread is a fair price for an uncertain, leverage-gated, market-conditions-contingent timeline, not a mispricing the market is offside on.
valley_of_death: Next 3–9 months: (1) Coffee drag — JDE Peet's integration + soft global coffee category pressures EPS and the multiple before the spin re-rates anything. (2) Leverage — ~4.5x combined net debt is the gating item; deleveraging is rate- and FCF-dependent and any miss pushes the spin window right and could pressure the credit/equity narrative. (3) No catalyst landing in the window — with separation 'likely early 2027,' a holder entering now likely sees NO spin event in the next 9 months, so the position is dead money / pure carry (the 3% dividend) absent a multiple re-rate, with headline risk on any further timeline slip. No cash-burn or maturity-wall cliff (staple FCF + dividend covered), but the absence of a near-term forcing event is the real valley.
positioning_washout: Limited mechanical-selling dynamic on the BUY side: KDP is a widely-held S&P 500 staple with a yield/quality shareholder base; the spin, when it happens, will FORCE passive re-weighting (a buy-side flow catalyst across two new tickers, including potential dividend-investor sorting between the higher-yield Beverage Co. and the growthier Global Coffee Co.). Pre-spin there is no dividend cut or forced-liquidation trigger to wash out the base — which also means there is no capitulation flush to buy into; entry is a patience trade, not a washout trade.
moderator_conclusion: KDP is a genuine, well-sponsored sum-of-parts spin whose HARD enabling leg (the ~$18B JDE Peet's acquisition) already CLOSED on Apr 1 2026 — removing the binary deal-break risk that kills most arb setups — but whose VALUE-UNLOCK event (the tax-free Global Coffee Co. spin + index-flow re-rate) is UNDATED, gated on ~4.5x deleveraging and 'supportive market conditions,' and has been serially right-shifting toward 'likely early 2027.' Under catalyst-regime discipline this is the textbook SOFT_EXTENDED case: real thesis, credible sponsor, but no hard date inside the window and only a modest, thinly-cushioned spread (R:R ~1.9, ~7.6% downside-to-floor). That is explicitly NOT an aggressive-entry / conviction-5 setup — those are reserved for dated, binding catalysts inside the window. It is also not a PASS: the floor is real (a profitable staple at ~13x with a covered 3% yield + buyback + asset-monetization optionality bounds the loss), the SoP is legitimate, and the asymmetry tilts favorable. VERDICT B — WATCHLIST/STAGE: wait for the spin to acquire a HARD date (Form 10 effectiveness / S&P reconstitution date) or for a leverage milestone that converts SOFT → PENDING_HARD; that conversion is the trigger to size up to an aggressive entry. value_conviction 3 = the margin of safety to the verified $28.50 floor is real but THIN (~$2.35), and it is a NARROW-moat staple, not a fortress — a fair, not generous, downside cushion. Conviction 3 / verdict B on the event asymmetry: credible but undated, mid-conviction by the rulebook.
interrogator_dossier:
# KDP — Keurig Dr Pepper — Interrogator Dossier (Event-Driven Special Situation)

**Frame:** NOT a quality/compounding thesis. KDP is judged as a sum-of-parts / spin + index-flow special situation: the spread between the live price (~$30.85, Jun 22 2026) and a separation-resolved value, gated on the tax-free spin of 'Global Coffee Co.' from 'Beverage Co.', with a floor set by the standalone/recovery value of the combined enterprise if the spin slips or breaks.

## 1. THE EVENT — REAL, DATED, BINDING?
**Real: YES.** Separation into two U.S.-listed pure-plays — Beverage Co. (NA refreshment: Dr Pepper, Snapple, Canada Dry, Mott's, energy/sports) and Global Coffee Co. (Keurig + acquired JDE Peet's). Structure = tax-free spin of Global Coffee Co. (clean index-flow form). **Acquisition leg: FIRED** — the ~$18B JDE Peet's acquisition CLOSED Apr 1 2026 (~$9B debt / ~$8.5B equity incl. $4.5B Bev Co. convertible preferred + ~$7B PE raise / ~$5B assumed JDE bonds). The binary deal-break risk is GONE. **Dated: NO — the load-bearing weakness.** The spin is UNDATED: per Q1 2026 call (Apr 23 2026) operational readiness end-2026, official separation 'likely early 2027,' explicitly conditioned on 'appropriate leverage levels at each company AND supportive market conditions.' No PDUFA-style date, no fixed proxy/effective/Form-10 date. **Binding: PARTIALLY** — board committed + sunk cost raise the abandonment bar, but 'supportive market conditions' is a real out-clause.

## 2. SPONSOR CREDIBILITY
Both CEOs already named (Cofer → Beverage Co.; Oliveira → Global Coffee Co.) — credible pre-spin commitment. Financing executed cleanly + JDE closed on schedule (delivered the hard part). Q1 2026 reaffirmed low-double-digit EPS growth (EPS $1.35 +22.7%, rev TTM $16.94B +9.2%); the operating business funds the deleveraging. Counter: every prior communication right-shifted the date (year-end 2026 → 'operational readiness' end-2026 → 'likely early 2027') — the DATE is slipping even as the PLAN strengthens.

## 3. VERIFIED DOWNSIDE FLOOR
Floor is NOT a deal-break (deal closed) — it is the standalone/recovery value if the spin delays. ~$30.85 = forward P/E ~12.9x on a staple cash machine, 3% covered dividend, reaffirmed double-digit EPS growth. A staple at 13–14x forward with a safe yield + buyback + non-core asset monetization optionality supports the price. Floor $28.50 (~7.6% below live) is a defensible no-catalyst anchor — REAL but THIN (~$2.35 cushion).

## 4. RED FLAGS
🔴 Catalyst UNDATED + serially right-shifting ('likely early 2027,' market-conditions out-clause) — SOFT, not PENDING_HARD. 🔴 ~4.5x combined net leverage — spin is GATED on deleveraging at BOTH entities (rate/FCF-dependent). 🔴 Coffee drag (JDE integration + soft category) pressures near-term EPS/multiple. 🔴 Modest spread: R:R 1.87, ~22% upside vs ~7.6% downside — thin pay for an undated, possibly 12–18-mo wait.

## 5. GREEN FLAGS
🟢 HARD enabling leg already fired (JDE close Apr 1 2026) — no deal-break risk. 🟢 Tax-free spin = clean index-flow SoP (mechanical passive re-weighting + pure-play re-rate). 🟢 Both CEOs named, financing closed, EPS guidance reaffirmed — high execution credibility, not vaporware. 🟢 Defended floor (profitable staple, covered 3% yield, buyback).

## 6. EVENT VERDICT
Genuine well-sponsored SoP spin whose enabling transaction has fired, but whose value-unlock is UNDATED, leverage/market-conditions-contingent, and slipping toward early 2027. Asymmetry real but modest (~1.9:1), wait long with a soft clock. High-quality WATCH/staging special-sit — NOT a fire-now PENDING_HARD trigger.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 3 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
skeptic_kill_fact: The 'catalyst' is NOT a dated forcing event — it is an UNDATED sum-of-parts wait. Verified against KDP's own IR: the tax-free spin of Global Coffee Co. has 'exact timing... yet to be determined,' is gated on 'appropriate leverage levels at each company, and supportive market conditions' (a real out-clause), with operational readiness only TARGETED by year-end 2026 and separation merely 'likely early 2027.' There is no Form 10, no record/effective date, no S&P reconstitution date, no hard clock inside any near-term window. The HARD leg (JDE Peet's) already fired Apr 1 2026, so there is NO live arb spread to capture — the deal-break asymmetry is gone and what remains is a patient SoP/re-rate trade, not an event with a binding date. The $28.50 'floor' is a valuation anchor (staple at ~13x + covered ~3% yield), NOT a backstopped recovery/cash value — it can break in a multiple de-rate or a coffee-drag EPS miss. Asymmetry survives (real SoP, real-but-thin floor, R:R ~1.9), but only at low conviction: this is a WATCH/stage setup, not a fire-now dated catalyst.

catalyst.dated_milestone: JDE Peet's acquisition CLOSED Apr 1, 2026 (hard leg fired). Spin: operational readiness targeted end-2026; official separation 'likely early 2027' per Q1 2026 call (Apr 23, 2026) — UNDATED, conditioned on leverage + supportive market conditions. No fixed record/effective/Form-10 date in hand.
catalyst.binding_reason: Tax-free spin of Global Coffee Co. — the HARD enabling leg (JDE Peet's) already CLOSED 4/1/26, so there is NO live arb spread left to capture; what remains is an UNDATED SoP wait, gated on ~4.5x deleveraging + an explicit 'supportive market conditions' out-clause, separation merely 'likely early 2027.' Skeptic CONFIRMED, cap 3 (~60), status SOFT_EXTENDED. Modest, thinly-cushioned spread (R:R ~1.9, ~7.6% to a valuation-anchor floor that is not recovery-backed). Real but undated; stage only when the spin acquires a hard Form-10/record date.

bull_thesis: The trade is a classic break-up, or sum-of-the-parts, story: the belief that Keurig Dr Pepper is worth more split in two than it is as one company. On April 1, 2026 the company closed an $18.4 billion purchase of JDE Peet's, the European coffee giant behind Jacobs, L'OR, Peet's and Douwe Egberts, assembling what would become the world's largest pure-play coffee company. It then committed to spinning the whole group into two separately listed firms: a North American refreshment-beverage company (Dr Pepper, 7UP, Snapple, Canada Dry, about $11 billion in annual net sales) and a global coffee company (Keurig, Green Mountain plus the JDE Peet's brands, about $16 billion in net sales). The bull case is that focused, separately-listed companies attract their own dedicated shareholders and, critically, force index funds to buy each new ticker when it joins the major stock indexes, a mechanical wave of buying the conglomerate's single listing does not capture today. At roughly $33 a share against a sum-of-the-parts value in the high-$30s, the bull collects that gap plus a 2.75 percent dividend while waiting. For this to pay, the spin has to actually complete on something close to the stated timetable, the two pieces have to be valued on the higher multiples that focused peers earn, and the heavy post-deal debt (combined net leverage around 4.5 times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization) has to come down enough to let the separation proceed cleanly.
bear_thesis: The catalyst is soft and has already slipped. When the deal was announced the split was guided for late 2026; as of the company's June 23, 2026 update it has moved to early 2027, and it remains explicitly conditioned on reaching 'appropriate leverage levels' and 'supportive market conditions' rather than any fixed date. A spin with no record date and no effective date is a corporate intention the board can delay or restructure, not a binding event. The near-term execution picture also wobbled: on June 23, 2026 the executive picked to run the future coffee company, Rafa Oliveira, announced he is leaving at the end of July for an outside chief-executive role, forcing a fresh CEO search for the larger of the two spin entities weeks before it is meant to stand up. The balance sheet is levered after funding the acquisition with about $9 billion of new debt and assuming roughly $5 billion of JDE Peet's bonds, which both narrows the margin of safety and is itself the gating constraint on the timetable. If the split drifts into late 2027 or is quietly reshaped, the index-flow re-rating never fires and the stock simply trades as a fully-valued, levered staples name at a price-to-earnings ratio already around 25. The spread closes by time decay, not by a payoff.
consensus_delta: The disagreement with the market is narrow here, which is itself the verdict. The bull view is that the street under-prices the mechanical index-fund buying that a two-way spin unleashes when each new company joins the major indexes. But that argument is weakened by the facts on the ground: the stock has already climbed from the mid-$20s toward $33, roughly the middle of its 52-week range, so much of the optimism is in the price. With a base-case sum-of-the-parts near $33 to $34, the market is already pricing the spin as a coin-flip rather than ignoring it. The genuine variant view would be that the spin completes faster and cleaner than the recent slippage and CEO departure suggest — but the evidence points the other way, so there is little durable mispricing to harvest at today's level.
forcing_function: There is no hard external clock — that is the problem. The re-rating only fires when the board declares a concrete spin record/effective date and the two companies list separately, triggering index-inclusion buying. The watch-for trigger is a firm dated separation announcement plus a stabilized Global Coffee Co. CEO appointment; absent those, nothing forces the gap to close.
valley_of_death: The next three to nine months carry the soft-catalyst risks rather than a cash crisis. KDP is comfortably cash-generative and pays its dividend, so there is no burn hump or near-term maturity wall threatening the floor. The real near-term hazards are timeline and execution: the separation target has already slipped from late 2026 to early 2027 and could slip again, and the designated coffee-company chief executive leaves at the end of July 2026, leaving the larger spin entity without a permanent leader during the run-up. The ~4.5x leverage is the gating constraint — management must keep deleveraging on track, and any disappointment there or a souring of 'market conditions' would push the spin out and let the spread decay. None of this decimates free cash flow; it simply delays the payoff and risks the stock drifting back toward its floor.
positioning_washout: Limited forced-selling dynamics. KDP is a stable, dividend-paying staples holding owned largely by income and index investors who have no mechanical reason to liquidate ahead of the spin; if anything, a completed split creates forced buying (index inclusion of the new tickers), not forced selling. There is no dividend cut or strategy pivot that would flush the existing base. The flip side is that there is no capitulation flush to buy into either — the shareholder base is calm, the price is mid-range, and there is no wash-out setting up a cheaper entry.
moderator_conclusion: Keurig Dr Pepper is a sound break-up idea wrapped around a catalyst that is no longer dated or imminent, bought at a price that has already done much of the work. The strategic logic is real: assemble the world's largest pure-play coffee company, then split it from a clean North American soda business so each can be valued and indexed on its own. But three things move this off an aggressive-entry footing. First, the catalyst is soft and slipping — the separation has gone from 'late 2026' to 'early 2027,' has no record or effective date, and is explicitly contingent on deleveraging and market conditions. Second, execution just wobbled: the executive chosen to lead the larger spin entity is leaving at the end of July 2026, forcing a new CEO search. Third, and most decisive for sizing, the stock has already rallied to about $33, the middle of its 52-week range, so the reward-to-risk from here is only about 0.9-to-1 against a $37.7 target and a $28.5 floor — the easy money was made on the way up from the mid-$20s. The real business under the trade is a genuine, dividend-paying floor, which keeps this far from a value trap, but a defined-risk special situation only earns an aggressive bet when the date is hard and the price is well below the event value. Here the date is soft and the price is mid-stream. This is a watch-list name: wait for a firm, dated separation announcement and stabilized coffee-company leadership — or a pullback toward the floor — before committing capital.
sop_breakdown: Probability-weighted between the event-win value (~$37.7) and the business-only floor (~$28.5). Giving the spin a roughly 55 percent chance of completing near the stated early-2027 timetable and re-rating, against a 45 percent chance it slips further or is reshaped and the stock holds near its floor, yields a base-case sum-of-the-parts of about $33 to $34 a share, essentially where the stock trades today. In other words the market is already pricing a coin-flip on the spin, which is why the edge is moderate rather than large.
risk_reward: Upside to target about $4.4 a share (from ~$33.3 to ~$37.7); downside to floor about $4.8 a share (from ~$33.3 to ~$28.5). Reward-to-risk roughly 0.9-to-1 from today's price, well below the dossier-board's 1.87 figure because the stock has already rallied toward the middle of its range; the asymmetry is no longer compelling at $33.
interrogator_dossier:
# Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) — Forensic Dossier (Event-Driven Special Situation)

*Prepared by the Interrogator. Reviewed as of June 29, 2026. The thesis on the table is not "is this a great business" — it is whether a corporate break-up unlocks more value than the live price reflects, on a firm enough timetable to bet on.*

## The setup in one paragraph

Keurig Dr Pepper is a roughly $45 billion North American beverage-and-coffee group (Dr Pepper, 7UP, Snapple, Canada Dry on the soda side; Keurig, Green Mountain on the coffee side). On April 1, 2026 it closed an $18.4 billion acquisition of JDE Peet's, the European coffee giant (Jacobs, L'OR, Peet's, Douwe Egberts), and announced that once the combined coffee business was assembled it would split the whole company into two separately listed firms: a North American refreshment-beverage company (about $11 billion in annual net sales) and the world's largest pure-play coffee company (about $16 billion in net sales). The investment case here is the classic "sum-of-the-parts" trade — the belief that two focused companies, valued separately and picked up by index funds that must own each new ticker, are worth more than the conglomerate trades for today. The catalyst that is supposed to force that re-rating is the spin itself plus the buying pressure when each new company enters the indexes.

## 1. Is the catalyst real, dated, and binding?

Real: yes. This is a board-approved, publicly committed plan, not a rumor. Management has named the two companies, assigned leadership, and is actively building the separation.

Dated: no — and this is the crux. The spin has no record date and no effective date. Worse, the goalposts have already moved. When the deal was announced, management guided to a separation in "late 2026," with teams targeting "operational readiness to separate by year-end 2026." As of the company's June 23, 2026 update, the target has slipped to "early 2027." A catalyst that was a few months out at the start of the year is now a year out, and the language remains soft — the separation is explicitly conditioned on reaching "appropriate leverage levels at each company" and "supportive market conditions." Those are management's words for "we will do this when the balance sheet and the market let us," which is the opposite of a hard, contractual date.

Binding: only loosely. A spin is a corporate decision the board can delay, restructure, or pause if conditions sour. There is no counterparty, no merger agreement with a drop-dead date, no regulator on a clock, no contractual payout. Unlike a merger arbitrage (where signed terms cap and floor the outcome) or a drug-approval date (where a regulator must rule by a deadline), nothing external forces KDP to complete this split on any particular day.

## 2. Credibility of management on hitting the timetable

Mixed, and trending the wrong way in the very near term. Two concrete red flags:

- The timeline has already slipped once (late-2026 to early-2027) inside the first quarter after the deal closed. Serial slippage is the signature of a soft catalyst.
- On June 23, 2026, the executive picked to run the future coffee company — Rafa Oliveira, the JDE Peet's veteran heading the coffee operating unit — announced he is leaving at the end of July 2026 for an outside chief-executive job. The board has had to open a fresh search for the Global Coffee Co. chief executive, led by the chairman. Losing the named leader of the larger of the two spin entities, months before the spin, is a governance and execution wobble, not a confidence signal. Existing CEO Tim Cofer is back-filling oversight of coffee during the transition and will run the beverage company after the split.

On the other hand, the strategic logic is sound and the financing is in place: the acquisition was funded with roughly $9 billion of new long-term debt, about $8.5 billion of equity capital (including a $4.5 billion convertible preferred from Apollo, KKR and T. Rowe Price), and the assumption of about $5 billion of JDE Peet's bonds. The plan is internally coherent. The issue is timing risk, not whether the company intends to do it.

## 3. The verified downside floor

This is where KDP differs from a typical "cheap-and-bad" special situation — there is a real business under the trade, which is what backstops the price if the spin drags. KDP is a profitable, dividend-paying consumer-staples company: about $45 billion market value, a price-to-earnings ratio around 25, a dividend yield of roughly 2.75 percent ($0.92 a year), and a 52-week range of about $24.88 to $35.94. Even if the spin is delayed a year or quietly restructured, you still own a cash-generative staples franchise paying you to wait. The realistic floor is the "no-catalyst, just-the-business" value — the lower part of the recent trading range, around the high-$20s, where the stock found support over the past year and where the dividend yield alone would draw buyers. The dossier-board floor of $28.5 is consistent with that: it is roughly the recent support zone, not a distressed liquidation price.

The caveat: the post-deal balance sheet is levered (combined net leverage around 4.5x earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization). A staples business can carry that, but it removes some of the cushion and is precisely why management is gating the spin on "appropriate leverage levels" — they need to pay debt down first. Higher leverage means the floor is firmer than a broken biotech but softer than a net-cash situation.

## 4. Red flags

- Undated, serially-slipping catalyst (late-2026 to early-2027 already).
- Spin explicitly conditioned on leverage and "market conditions" — discretionary, not binding.
- Departure of the designated coffee-company chief executive weeks before he was to be installed; open CEO search.
- Elevated leverage (~4.5x) post-acquisition narrows the margin of safety and is itself the gating constraint on the spin.

## 5. Green flags

- Genuine, board-committed sum-of-the-parts story with a credible value-unlock logic (the world's #1 pure-play coffee company plus a clean North American soda business).
- Real, profitable, dividend-paying business as the floor — you are not paying for a lottery ticket on a shell.
- Financing fully arranged; sophisticated capital partners (Apollo, KKR, T. Rowe) underwrote the preferred.
- The spread is modest but real: live price about $33 versus a sum-of-the-parts target near $38.

## 6. Capital-allocation verdict for the director

This is a sound break-up thesis attached to a soft, undated, recently-slipped catalyst. The asymmetry is fine but not dramatic — roughly $33 today against a high-$30s target and a high-$20s floor, a reward-to-risk a touch under two-to-one. The single most important thing to weigh before sizing: do not treat this like a dated, binding special situation. There is no hard date, the date has already moved once, and the leader of the larger spin-entity just walked. This belongs on the watch list, sized for patience, with the trigger being a concrete record/effective date for the spin and stabilized coffee-company leadership — not bought as if a clock were ticking.

On the standard quality and moat axes (mostly not the point here): the underlying franchise is a wide, stable consumer-brand moat with no terminal secular threat — soda and single-serve coffee are durable, slow-growth categories. But the trade is the event, not the moat, so those tags are secondary.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 3 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: WIDE | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: none
